title: Cap-exhaustion refusal can SIGKILL a heartbeating-alive daemon (reap gate ignores lease heartbeat)

findingDetails.evidence: At cap the server closes the socket with no JSON-RPC reply (socket-server.ts:303-307); probeDaemon reads that as 'dead'/closed-before-reply (launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:227-229); the N-retry mitigation (launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:348-357) cannot recover because every retry reconnects into the still-full cap; action:'respawn' (launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:421-424) reaches reapOwnerBeforeRespawn, whose only liveness gate is processLiveness — a running, heartbeating owner is not 'dead', so it falls through to SIGTERM/SIGKILL (mk-spec-memory-launcher.cjs:759-771). Pre-reap guards check only that the lease pid is unchanged (:807-818), never lastHeartbeatIso/ttlMs, though classifyOwnerLease:433-444 already computes that staleness.
findingDetails.why: The cap raise to 64 is a threshold mitigation of the documented refused==dead indistinguishability (socket-server.ts:18-20), but does not remove it: once 64 clients hold slots, an arriving launcher misreads cap-refusal as death and can kill a healthy daemon serving 64 sessions, aborting in-flight SQLite/FTS work. The retry mitigation was tuned for a busy daemon mid-FTS-merge (which eventually replies); cap exhaustion never lets the daemon reply, so retries don't cover it.
findingDetails.recommendation: Before reaping, require the owner lease to be heartbeat-stale (reuse classifyOwnerLease's lastHeartbeatIso/ttlMs check), not merely pid-alive. A daemon that has heartbeated within its TTL must never be reaped on a socket-probe-only 'dead' verdict, regardless of cap.

title: Liveness probes share the client cap, amplifying false-reap below 64 real sessions

findingDetails.evidence: Every accepted connection — including deep-probe liveness connections — is added to activeSockets and spins up a full createServer() (socket-server.ts:310,328), holding a slot from connect until the probe destroys the socket post-reply (launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:178,227). The cap check at :303 counts these probe slots identically to durable bridge slots.
findingDetails.why: A synchronized launcher burst (large parallel fan-out probing at once) can transiently saturate the 64-slot cap with probe traffic alone, triggering the false-reap path with far fewer than 64 long-lived sessions. The documented 'cap must exceed realistic session fleet' rationale (socket-server.ts:18-20) reasons only about durable sessions, not probe bursts, so the mitigation's safety margin is smaller than stated.
findingDetails.recommendation: Exempt liveness probes from the client cap (e.g. answer the initialize probe via a lightweight pre-accept path that does not occupy a durable slot, or count probe vs bridge connections separately), so transient probe bursts cannot exhaust the cap intended for real sessions.
